word: G2139 - εὐπερίστατος - euperístatos - yoo-per-is'-tat-os - Which doth so easily beset

sections.paragraph:
- Original: εὐπερίστατος

- Transliteration: Euperistatos

- Phonetic: yoo-per-is'-tat-os

- Definition:

1. skilfully surrounding i.e. besetting

- Origin: from G2095 and a derivative of a presumed compound of G4012 and G2476

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From G2095 and a derivative of a presumed compound of G4012 and G2476; well standing around that is (a competitor) thwarting (a racer) in every direction (figuratively of sin in general): - which doth so easily beset.
